business_justification: MiFID II and SEC regulatory frameworks impose conflicting requirements for certain transaction reporting and market structure rules. Full compliance with both regimes simultaneously is not technically feasible for specific derivative instrument classifications. External counsel (Clifford Chance LLP) provided analysis confirming no enforcement action precedent for the specific divergence area. Regulatory harmonization discussions ongoing at IOSCO level.
compensating_controls: 1. Dedicated dual compliance team (3 EU + 2 US compliance analysts) monitoring both regimes; 2. External counsel retainer with Clifford Chance LLP for ongoing regulatory interpretation; 3. Automated regulatory monitoring dashboard (RegTech: Ascent) scanning for changes; 4. Quarterly compliance attestation signed by Chief Compliance Officer; 5. Pre-trade compliance checks cover both regimes where overlap exists
cro_recommendation: Andrew Park (CRO) - Recommended approval with compensating controls. Noted residual risk is bounded by insurance and monitoring.
quarterly_reviews: Q3 2025 (Oct 8): Reviewed by Risk Committee. No regulatory changes. Status: Compliant with compensating controls.; Q4 2025 (Dec 18): Reviewed by Risk Committee. IOSCO harmonization draft published. Clifford Chance monitoring. Status: Compliant.
insurance_coverage: AIG Policy FIN-2025-NF-0091 covers regulatory fines up to $15M
